silva_138_amplicon.source: SILVA release files converted for the selected amplicon backend.
silva_138_amplicon.license_note: Respect SILVA distribution and citation requirements.
silva_138_amplicon.estimated_size: medium; depends on release and classifier representation
suggested_setup: Place release-matched taxonomy.tsv and sequences.fasta under "$NGS_DB_SILVA_138_ROOT".
For QIIME2 classify-sklearn, also track the trained .qza classifier path used for the run.

gtdb_release.source: GTDB release bundle prepared for the selected classifier.
gtdb_release.license_note: Track GTDB release and classifier-specific conversion details.
gtdb_release.estimated_size: medium to large; depends on GTDB release and classifier representation
suggested_setup: Place release-matched taxonomy.tsv and sequences.fasta under "$NGS_DB_GTDB_ROOT".
Record the GTDB release, classifier backend, and conversion command in the run resource manifest.

kraken2_standard.source: Kraken2 standard database or lab-curated equivalent.
kraken2_standard.license_note: Large database downloads should be deliberate and checksum-tracked.
kraken2_standard.estimated_size: large; Kraken2 standard databases can require substantial disk space

bracken_standard.source: Bracken files generated from the exact Kraken2 database used for classification.
bracken_standard.license_note: Bracken abundance estimates are only valid when read length and Kraken database match the generated kmer distribution.
bracken_standard.estimated_size: small to medium relative to the paired Kraken2 database
suggested_setup: Use the exact Kraken2 database root used for classification.
Keep one kmer distribution per read length when workflows mix read lengths.

humann_uniref90.display_name: HUMAnN UniRef90 and ChocoPhlAn database bundle
humann_uniref90.source: HUMAnN utility downloads or local mirrored database bundle.
humann_uniref90.license_note: HUMAnN databases are large and should be versioned with the HUMAnN software release.
humann_uniref90.estimated_size: large; ChocoPhlAn plus UniRef databases can require substantial disk space
